channel.title: GeoPulse. Geopolitics in brief
channel.description: Signal-first briefings on geopolitics, edited by Lavkesh Dwivedi.

item.title: Israel-Lebanon Talks Resume Amid U.S.-Iran Impasse
item.description: Israel and Lebanon resumed talks. The US and Iran remain at an impasse. Israel-Lebanon Talks Resume Amid U. S.-Iran Impasse Council on Foreign Relations.

item.title: Trump orders U.S. military to 'shoot and kill' Iranian small boats choking Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Trump ordered the US military to shoot Iranian boats in the Strait of Hormuz. Pentagon blasted as cherry picking and false a news report saying that the dept. assessed it could take six months to completely clear the Strait of Hormuz of Iranian-laid mines.

item.title: US-Israel-Iran War Ceasefire News Live Updates: 'Hardliners vs moderates', Trump claims 'crazy'
item.description: Iran received its first revenue from tolls collected at the Strait of Hormuz. The payment was made after a recent agreement. The official did not disclose the revenue amount. US-Israel-Iran War Ceasefire News Live Updates: Iran receives first revenue from Strait of Hormuz tolls, says official The Times of India.

item.title: Trump Threatens Iranian Boats He Says Are Mining the Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Oil prices hovered above $100 a barrel as reported ship seizures added to market anxiety. The White House said President Trump does not view Iran's reported seizures as a cease-fire violation.

item.title: Trump orders U.S. Navy to 'shoot, kill' small boats laying mines in Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Pakistan's minister and a US envoy discussed efforts for a second round of US-Iran talks. Iran’s vow not to reopen the Strait of Hormuz so long as a U. S. naval blockade remained in place.

item.title: US host Hungary, France face Australia in BJK Cup playoffs
item.description: The US hosts Hungary and France faces Australia in the BJK Cup playoffs. US host Hungary, France face Australia in BJK Cup playoffs.

item.title: Trump says 'no time frame' for ending Iran war as fragile ceasefire holds
item.description: Trump stated there is no time frame for ending the Iran war. Trump says 'no time frame' for ending Iran war as fragile ceasefire holds WJLA.

item.title: Amid US Blockade, Iran Says It Has Banked First Proceeds From Hormuz Tolls
item.description: Iran banked its first proceeds from Hormuz tolls amid a US blockade. Iran vowed it will keep the strait closed to all but a trickle of approved vessels. Amid US Blockade, Iran Says It Has Banked First Proceeds From Hormuz Tolls.

item.title: Pentagon Denies Reports Claiming Hormuz Mine Clearance Will Take 6 Months
item.description: The Pentagon denied reports that mine clearance in the Hormuz Strait would take six months. Some mines use GPS technology to float remotely, making them harder to detect. Some mines floated remotely using GPS technology that makes them harder to detect.

item.title: Spotlight On $17-Billion Trump-Class Warship After Pentagon Axes Navy Chief
item.description: The Pentagon axed Navy Chief after issues with a $17-billion warship, the Trump-class vessel. An image generated by AI brought the warship under scrutiny. Spotlight On $17-Billion Trump-Class Warship After Pentagon Axes Navy Chief.

item.title: Trump reposts critique targeting India, China over birthright citizenship
item.description: US President Trump reposted a critique targeting India and China over birthright citizenship. The Indian MEA said it had seen reports, while the US Embassy spokesperson praised India. MEA says it has “seen reports”; U. S.

item.title: ‘Solutions, not slogans’: Gaza holds first election in 21 years
item.description: Gaza held its first local election in 21 years in Deir el-Balah, with residents demanding real solutions amid war ruins. The election was seen as a step towards stability. Deir el-Balah holds Gaza's first local election in 21 years amid war ruins, with residents demanding real solutions.

item.title: Iran ceasefire holds but tensions escalate on the water: Live updates
item.description: The Iran ceasefire held, but tensions escalated on the water. The situation remains volatile, with live updates indicating ongoing developments in the region. Iran ceasefire holds but tensions escalate on the water: Live updates USA Today.

item.title: Daily life shadowed by war, scarcity and fear
item.description: The US naval blockade of Iran is affecting ordinary Iranians, who face growing exhaustion in their daily lives. Many struggle to cope with the consequences of the blockade, which has become a source of widespread hardship.

item.title: ‘The world’s most impossible job’: Who will lead the United Nations next?
item.description: The United Nations is searching for a new leader after the current secretary-general's term ends. France and other countries are reportedly considering candidates for the position, which is seen as one of the most challenging jobs in international diplomacy.

item.title: US Senate passes $70bn funding plan for ICE, Border Patrol
item.description: The US Senate has passed a $70 billion funding plan for Immigration and Customs Enforcement and the Border Patrol. The plan, which was approved with a 50-48 vote, aims to reopen the Department of Homeland Security and provide additional resources for border security.

item.title: St Vincent and Grenadines government pauses constitutional amendment bills after public backlash
item.description: The St Vincent and the Grenadines government has delayed two constitutional amendment bills in response to public backlash. The bills aimed to address election petitions challenging the eligibility of two politicians to serve in parliament due to dual citizenship.

item.title: Infiltration from Within: Israelis recruited to spy for enemy countries
item.description: Israel's intelligence agency has revealed that several Israelis have been recruited to spy for enemy countries. The revelation highlights the country's vulnerability to internal espionage and raises concerns about national security in the wake of recent conflicts.

item.title: Pakistan Astronauts To Train For China's Tiangong Space Station In Historic First
item.description: Pakistan astronauts will train for China's Tiangong space station, marking a milestone in China's space programme. This historic first highlights growing cooperation between China and Pakistan. Beijing described the development as a milestone in its space programme.

item.title: Russia says it's maintaining oil supplies but has no new OPEC+ initiatives
item.description: Russia's Kremlin spokesperson Dmitry Peskov stated that the country continues to maintain its oil supplies. Russia is contributing to global price stability and minimizing energy crisis impacts. “At ‌the moment, we are making our ‌contribution ⁠to stabilizing prices and minimizing the consequences ⁠of the global energy crisis.
